skill.name: startup-analyst
skill.description: Startup analysis assistant for market sizing, financial modeling, competitive research, metrics, hiring plans, and fundraising preparation.
skill.summary: Analyze startup markets, economics, competitors, metrics, and fundraising plans.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: AI review found the static analyzer alerts to be false positives caused by business acronyms, Markdown inline code, and ordinary prose. No prompt injection, executable script, credential access, cryptography use, system reconnaissance, or malicious intent was found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Inline Commands
low_findings.description: Lines 28 and 230-232 use Markdown inline code for a resource path and plugin slash-command names. They are not Ruby shell backticks, shell execution, or dynamic command invocation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious tokens are enclosed as documentation examples, not executable code. No shell API, Ruby code, script file, or command interpolation is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Market Sizing SAM Acronym
low_findings.description: The SAM matches are startup market sizing terminology in TAM/SAM/SOM references. They do not refer to the Windows SAM database or credential material.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding text is explicitly about market opportunity calculations. There is no file access, Windows path, credential extraction, or database reference.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Crypto Keyword Matches
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts appear to come from substrings inside ordinary business words such as description, design, and commands. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing function, or encryption logic appears in the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found business-analysis prose at each flagged line. There are no crypto APIs, algorithm names used as algorithms, encoded payloads, or security-sensitive operations.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Non-Reconnaissance Business Guidance
low_findings.description: Line 127 discusses common startup-analysis pitfalls, and line 328 states the skill goal for founder guidance. Neither line instructs system reconnaissance or host enumeration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are plain business guidance. No commands, system paths, process inspection, network scanning, or environment discovery instructions are present.

content.user_title: Analyze Startup Markets and Financial Plans
content.value_statement: Founders need clear startup analysis before making fundraising, hiring, and market decisions. This skill structures market sizing, financial modeling, competitive research, and metrics into practical outputs.
actual_capabilities: Guides TAM, SAM, and SOM market sizing with bottom-up and top-down methods.
Builds startup financial planning logic for revenue, costs, burn rate, runway, and scenarios.
Frames competitive landscapes with positioning, differentiation, barriers, and market forces.
Recommends stage-aware hiring plans, compensation considerations, and organization sequencing.
Explains startup metrics such as CAC, LTV, payback period, burn multiple, and retention.
Produces structured investor-facing analysis with assumptions, benchmarks, sources, and next steps.
limitations: It does not include executable spreadsheets or built-in financial model files.
Current market data requires web access and source verification during the session.
Benchmarks may be US-focused and should be adjusted for other regions.
It provides business analysis, not legal, tax, accounting, or investment advice.

use_cases.title: Size a Fundraising Market Story
use_cases.description: Estimate TAM, SAM, and SOM for a new startup idea and turn the result into a defensible investor narrative.

use_cases.title: Stress-Test Startup Economics
use_cases.description: Review revenue assumptions, unit economics, burn rate, runway, and hiring plans before committing budget.

use_cases.title: Map a Competitive Landscape
use_cases.description: Compare competitors, market forces, differentiation, and risks before a diligence call or investment memo.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Market Size
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for market sizing structure
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the market opportunity for my startup. Ask me for the business model, target customer, geography, pricing, and adoption assumptions before estimating TAM, SAM, and SOM.

prompt_templates.title: Financial Model Assumptions
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create a three-year startup financial plan. Use revenue, headcount, gross margin, CAC, retention, burn rate, runway, and conservative, base, and optimistic scenarios.

prompt_templates.title: Competitive Positioning Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze my competitive landscape and positioning. Compare direct and indirect competitors, switching costs, barriers to entry, differentiation, pricing, and likely investor concerns.

prompt_templates.title: Investor-Ready Business Case
prompt_templates.prompt: Build an investor-ready analysis for my startup. Include market sizing, business model, unit economics, competitive risks, hiring plan, fundraising milestones, assumptions, and source gaps.

output_examples.input: Estimate the opportunity for a B2B SaaS tool serving construction project managers.
output: A market sizing section with TAM, SAM, and SOM assumptions.
A bottom-up customer count and pricing model.
A source list with publication dates and data quality notes.
A short investor narrative and risks to validate.

output_examples.input: Review whether our $2,500 CAC and $8,000 LTV are healthy for enterprise SaaS.
output: A unit economics review with CAC, LTV, LTV-to-CAC ratio, and payback period.
A benchmark comparison for the company stage and business model.
Recommendations for improving sales efficiency and retention.

output_examples.input: Plan our first hires after a seed round.
output: A staged hiring sequence tied to milestones and runway.
Role prioritization across product, engineering, sales, and operations.
Risks, tradeoffs, and decisions that should wait until traction improves.

best_practices: Provide company stage, business model, geography, customer segment, pricing, and current traction before asking for analysis.
Ask the skill to separate assumptions, calculations, benchmarks, risks, and recommendations in the output.
Request source dates and confidence levels when using market data for fundraising or board materials.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill to invent market data when reliable sources are unavailable.
Do not rely on optimistic top-down TAM alone without bottom-up validation.
Do not treat generated financial projections as accounting, tax, legal, or investment advice.

faq.question: What startup stages does this skill support?
faq.answer: It is designed for pre-seed, seed, and Series A companies that need practical analysis for planning and fundraising.

faq.question: Can it create TAM, SAM, and SOM estimates?
faq.answer: Yes. It guides top-down, bottom-up, and value-based methods, then documents assumptions and validation needs.

faq.question: Can it build a full spreadsheet model?
faq.answer: It can define model structure, assumptions, formulas, and scenarios, but it does not include a packaged spreadsheet file.

faq.question: Does it use current market research?
faq.answer: It can guide web research when tools are available. Users should verify sources, dates, and data quality before relying on outputs.

faq.question: Is it suitable for investor materials?
faq.answer: Yes. It can structure investor-ready analysis with assumptions, benchmarks, risks, and next steps for further validation.

faq.question: What information should I provide first?
faq.answer: Provide the product, customer segment, business model, geography, pricing, traction, funding stage, and the decision you need to make.
